Front: When was the German Empire created?
Back: 1871 after German victory in the Franco Prussian War

Front: Who became the first German Emperor?
Who was Otto von Bismarck?
What political system existed in the German Empire?
Back: An authoritarian monarchy with limited parliamentary power

Front: What was the Reichstag?
Why did the Reichstag have limited power?
Back: The Kaiser and Chancellor controlled government policy

Front: What was Bismarck's Kulturkampf?
Back: A campaign against the influence of the Catholic Church

Front: Why did Bismarck introduce the Kulturkampf?
Back: He feared Catholic loyalty to the Pope over the German state

Front: Why did the Kulturkampf fail?
What were the Anti Socialist Laws?
Why did Bismarck oppose socialism?
Back: He believed it threatened the stability of the empire

Front: What social reforms did Bismarck introduce?
Back: Health insurance accident insurance and pensions

Front: Why did Bismarck introduce social reforms?
Who dismissed Bismarck in 1890?
Why did Wilhelm II dismiss Bismarck?
Back: He wanted to rule personally and change policy

Front: What was Weltpolitik?
Back: Wilhelm II's policy of expanding German global influence

Front: Why did Germany build a large navy?
What alliance system existed before World War I?
Which alliances existed?
Back: The Triple Alliance and the Triple Entente

Front: What event triggered World War I?
Back: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand

Front: When did World War I begin?
Why did Germany support Austria Hungary?
Back: They were allies in the Triple Alliance

Front: What was the Schlieffen Plan?
Back: Germany's strategy to defeat France quickly

Front: Why did the Schlieffen Plan fail?
What type of warfare developed on the Western Front?
Why did the war become a stalemate?
Back: Both sides had strong defensive positions

Front: What happened to Germany's economy during the war?
What was the impact of the British naval blockade?
What happened during the German Revolution of 1918?
Back: The Kaiser abdicated and Germany became a republic

Front: Who became the first president of the Weimar Republic?
What was the Weimar Republic?
Back: Germany's democratic government after World War I

Front: What treaty ended World War I?
Why was the Treaty of Versailles unpopular in Germany?
Back: It imposed harsh penalties and territorial losses

Front: What was Article 231?
Back: The War Guilt Clause blaming Germany for the war

Front: What economic penalties did Germany face?
What political problem did the Weimar Republic face?
Back: Opposition from both left and right wing extremists

Front: What was the Spartacist Uprising?
Who led the Spartacist Uprising?
How did the government suppress the uprising?
What was the Kapp Putsch?
Back: A right wing attempt to overthrow the government

Front: When did the Kapp Putsch occur?
Why did the Kapp Putsch fail?
What caused hyperinflation in 1923?
Back: Germany printed money to pay reparations and striking workers

Front: What was the impact of hyperinflation?
Back: The German currency became nearly worthless

Front: Why did France occupy the Ruhr?
What was passive resistance?
Back: Workers refusing to cooperate with French occupation

Front: Who helped stabilise the economy in 1923?
What economic reform did Stresemann introduce?
What was the Dawes Plan?
When was the Dawes Plan introduced?
What was the impact of the Dawes Plan?
What were the Golden Years?
Back: A period of economic recovery from 1924 to 1929

Front: What cultural developments occurred in the Golden Years?
Back: Growth in art film and literature

Front: Why did the Weimar Republic remain politically unstable?
What was the Wall Street Crash?
Back: A financial collapse in the United States in 1929

Front: Why did the Wall Street Crash affect Germany?
Back: Germany relied heavily on American loans

Front: What was the impact of the Great Depression?
Why did extremist parties gain support?
What political party gained support in this period?
Who led the Nazi Party?
Why did the Nazis gain popularity?
Back: They promised economic recovery and strong leadership
